slides.title: The Dictionary Problem 📖
slides.content:
Often in JavaScript, we use standard objects as dictionaries (or HashMaps) to structurally map keys to values, like mapping a User ID (string) to their Data (object).

Typing highly dynamic, infinitely scalable dictionaries is natively messy without the `Record` utility.

slides.title: The `Record<Keys, Type>` Utility
slides.content: `Record` elegantly dynamically constructs an object type where the property keys are `Keys` and the property values are absolutely always `Type`.

quizData.explanation: Every single value in a Record MUST flawlessly fundamentally match the provided value Type (in this case, `{ age: number }`).

slides.title: Restricting Keys 🎯
slides.content: You don't have to use a generic `string` for your keys. You can forcefully explicitly restrict the keys to be a very specific union of strings!

slides.title: Course Complete! 🏆
slides.content: You have conquered TypeScript! By flawlessly mastering typing, interfaces, classes, generics, and utility types, you are elegantly perfectly equipped to expertly successfully build incredibly robust, perfectly scalable, explicit enterprise software explicitly smoothly!
